title: MFFC — the "private cone" of a node

steps.title: Goal: how many ANDs would die if we deleted f?
steps.narrative:
An MFFC (Maximum Fanout-Free Cone) of a node n is the set of internal AIG ANDs whose only consumer — directly or transitively — is n. They exist solely to feed n; remove n and they become unreachable.

This is the upper bound on the gain a replacement can deliver: rewrite/refactor/resub will compute it for every candidate cut.

panes.title: Algorithm
panes.body: Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig(f) runs a deref + re-ref pair (abcRefs.c:100). The deref pass decrements each fanin's count; whenever a count hits 0 the recursion enters that fanin and the node joins the MFFC. The re-ref pass restores all the counters.

steps.title: Boundary boost on cut leaves {a, b, c, d}
steps.narrative: Operators MFFC w.r.t. a cut: stop the cone at the cut's leaves rather than running back to PIs. The trick is to +1 each leaf's fanout count beforehand; their counts then can't drop to zero during the deref, so the recursion bottoms out at the cut.

steps.title: Deref f → counter = 1, mark f
steps.narrative: deref(f): count = 1, label f with the current TravId. Then walk into f's two fanins.

steps.title: Deref n8 (1 → 0) → recurse, then n5 (1 → 0), n6 (1 → 0)
steps.narrative: f.fanin0 = n8 hits 0 → recurse. Inside deref(n8), both its fanins (n6 and n7) hit 0 in turn. Each becomes a new MFFC member. Each of their fanins is a cut leaf — boundary-boosted, so they decrement (3→2 etc.) but stay positive: no recursion past the cut.

steps.title: Deref n9 (1 → 0) → recurse, count = 5
steps.narrative: Back at f, fanin1 = n9 hits 0 → recurse. n9's fanins (c, d) are cut leaves, decrement-but-no-recurse.

steps.title: Result: nNodesSaved = 5
steps.narrative:
Abc_NodeRefDeref returns 5; Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig then runs the re-reference pass along the same path to put every fanout count back to its original value, asserting both passes agree.

The five MFFC nodes carry the current TravId, which lets Abc_NodeIsTravIdCurrent(node) answer "is this node in the MFFC?" in O(1) for downstream code such as Dec_GraphToNetworkCount.

panes.title: Different cut → different MFFC
panes.body: If the cut were instead {a, c, d, n6} (rewrite picks this in the §3 trace), n6's boundary boost would protect it during deref. n6 stays alive, so the MFFC shrinks to 4 nodes (and nNodesSaved = 4 as the rewrite trace reports).
